title: wolfhsm: document with_xxx_key closure RAII pattern at crate level
description:
## Why
The `with_xxx_key` closure-based RAII pattern (e.g. `client.with_ecc_p256_key(|key, client| {...})`) is unfamiliar to RustCrypto users who expect RAII-drop types. A module-level doc comment in `crypto/mod.rs` explains the design, but there is no crate-level docs explaining the pattern and why it was chosen over RAII drop types.

## What needs to be done
- Add a crate-level doc comment in `lib.rs` (or a `README.md` linked from it) with a section explaining:
  - Why `&mut Client` is required for every operation (serial request-response socket)
  - Why key lifetime uses closures rather than RAII drop (borrow checker: can't hold `&mut Client` inside a struct that also needs `&mut Client` to evict)
  - Usage example for `with_ecc_p256_key`
  - How to use multiple keys in sequence

## Acceptance criteria
- `cargo doc -p wolfhsm` shows meaningful crate-level documentation
- The design rationale for `with_xxx_key` is visible at the top level
